name: rinna japanese-gpt-neox-3.6b
archDisplay: Dense GPT-NeoX transformer (36 layers, 2816 hidden size)
note: One of Japan's earliest openly released, MIT-licensed Japanese LLMs, launched alongside an instruction-tuned SFT variant the same day.
verificationNotes: Confirmed real via independent Japanese news coverage and technical write-ups (Ledge.ai, digital-shift.jp, Qiita, Zenn, Hatena blog posts dated around the release). 3.6B params, MIT license, dense 36-layer/2816-hidden GPT-NeoX architecture, and 2048-token (2K) context all corroborated across multiple independent sources; trained on ~312.5B tokens from Japanese CC-100/C4/Wikipedia, released alongside an instruction-sft dialogue variant the same day, matching the record's note. The original prtimes.jp specSource URL for releaseDate 404'd (dead link) and could not be verified directly (HuggingFace and prtimes pages also blocked automated fetch with 401s), so I replaced it with a working corroborating source (digital-shift.jp), which independently reports the same May 17-18, 2023 release window, 3.6B params, and MIT license. Kept releaseDate as 2023-05-17 since a Japanese blog post literally dated 2023/05/17 discusses trying the model that day. No results rows were present in the input to verify. ROUND-7 COMPLETENESS CHECK (2026-07-15): found one real benchmark result -- rank 6th of 6 on the original Yuzu AI Rakuda benchmark (35% win rate, GPT-3.5-judged pairwise Bradley-Terry, yuzu-ai/japanese-llm-ranking, published 2023-06-29) -- but deliberately NOT added under the corpus's 'rakuda' slug: every other rakuda row in this corpus uses the unrelated Shaberi framework's GPT-4-judged 0-10 absolute scale, a different judge model and a different scoring methodology entirely, so mixing them would corrupt cross-model comparability for that slug. No other public benchmark data found for this model after a widened, Japanese-language-inclusive search.
